reviews.review_description:
It's a good game, but the gameplay loop is very repetitive. You fly the dragon and throw things at the enemies, and then you land on an island. Get an upgrade, maybe feed the dragon, and then you get back on the dragon and start flying and throwing enemies again. The combat reminds me a bit like Harpagun.

I was hoping for some grandiose interactions with the dragon. It's cool to look at the dragon at the start, but there is no massive awe inspiring scale moments. Its just you flying it maybe petting it and feeding it. Then you fly it whilst throwing enemies around. Rinse and repeat.

Also, devs please help I defeated the big purple boss and then got an egg and then did the next island and jumped back on the dragon but I can't get past the first checkpoint after that island, you know after you get the supercharge. It just keeps flying in an empty sky with health popping up here and there.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! We are currently looking into the issue you’ve raised regarding game progression. As a workaround you could try reloading from previous checkpoint.

reviews.review_description: Looks like this could be a great game, but it made me motion sick‽ I'm a fairly experienced vr user, and I almost never get motion sick. I actually cannot remember the last time I was motion sick. I tried going through settings, but there isn't a faster turn speed setting, or better movement settings. Please note, those settings are there, they just are very slow on the highest setting. I tried going through the game without using the analogue sticks, but something else seems off. Idk what, but I did get motion sick. I don't know if it's the teleportation, or what (I never usually play games with teleportation only movement). I'll try again later to see if it's any better since it seems to be a new game and hopefully will get some updates.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear that you are having issues with motion sickness… We are currently looking at options to help relieve this.

reviews.review_description:
There's too much of the same here to make the game enjoyable. There's a lot of rigidity when it comes to how it plays out. You walk around a small island then go into a space to get a new "upgrade" you'll learn to use your powers and do things like grab and throw, generate energy blobs, and crush your enemies. 
Your enemies here are basically globs of jelly with eyes and teeth. They can also shoot stuff at you which you can use on them. 
The controls are not very intuitive and "throwing" is actually just pulling the trigger. The game is very basic overall. 
Theres also no real story here, you just walk to your sky tail a flying creature that's either a feathery dragon or weird faced bird, and feed it and then a giant eye blob shows up. 
The graphics are good though so take at least that win 

Sort of a shame that the developers follow-up for the MIB game is this.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! We are sorry to hear that you’re not finding the game as enjoyable as we’d hoped.

reviews.review_description: Okay I find it awesome ! It's in early access and it's only $11. Give it time to grow. It's one of my favorites already. I'm sure more content will be added. Good work devs!
